metadata.source: Paul Jerimy Security Certification Roadmap + additional research
metadata.note: Lightweight index for 200+ security certs. For detailed info use get_cert_facts() or search_knowledge_base().

certs.brief: Foundational AWS certification covering core cloud concepts, AWS services, security basics, pricing, and billing. Suitable for business, management, and technical roles new to AWS.

Professional-level AWS certification validating expertise in provisioning, operating, and managing distributed application systems on AWS using DevOps methodologies including CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and monitoring.
One of the most popular cloud certifications, validating ability to design resilient, cost-efficient, and secure architectures on AWS. Covers core AWS services and architectural best practices.

Professional-level AWS credential validating advanced skills in designing complex, scalable, and cost-optimized AWS architectures. Covers advanced AWS services, migrations, and organizational strategies.
Specialty-level AWS certification validating expertise in data protection, incident response, infrastructure security, identity and access management, and logging and monitoring on AWS.
Validates skills in deploying, managing, and operating scalable, highly available systems on AWS. Targets system administrators responsible for managing and maintaining AWS environments.
Core Microsoft Azure administrator certification covering managing identities, governance, storage, compute, and virtual networks. A baseline credential for Azure infrastructure roles.
Expert-level Microsoft certification for designing cloud and hybrid solutions on Azure including infrastructure, identity, data storage, business continuity, and security architecture.
Validates skills in implementing security controls and threat protection, managing identity and access, and protecting data, applications, and networks in Microsoft Azure environments.

Entry-level Microsoft certification covering core Azure cloud concepts, services, pricing, and support. Ideal for non-technical roles or those new to cloud computing seeking a foundational credential.
Vendor-neutral certification covering cloud concepts, cloud business value, and the impact of cloud adoption on IT service management. Targets IT professionals and business stakeholders new to cloud.
Vendor-neutral cloud infrastructure certification covering deployment, security, operations, support, and troubleshooting in multi-cloud environments. Recognized across multiple cloud platforms.
Associate-level Google Cloud certification validating the ability to deploy and manage applications, monitor operations, and configure cloud solutions using Google Cloud tools and services.

certs.full_name: Google Cloud Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer
certs.brief: Professional-level Google Cloud certification validating skills in building and implementing DevOps practices including CI/CD pipelines, site reliability engineering (SRE), service monitoring, and infrastructure automation on GCP.

certs.full_name: Google Cloud Professional Cloud Security Engineer
certs.brief: Professional-level Google Cloud security certification covering designing and implementing secure infrastructure, managing access, protecting data, and monitoring for threats on GCP.

Legacy Microsoft associate-level certification series (retired 2021) covering Windows Server, SQL Server, Office 365, and other Microsoft technologies. Widely held credential still recognized in the industry.

Legacy Microsoft expert-level certification series (retired 2021) covering cloud platforms, data management, productivity, mobility, and security. Still widely recognized and held in the enterprise sector.
Covers securing Microsoft 365 enterprise environments including identity, access, threat protection, information protection, and compliance. Targets admins managing Microsoft 365 security posture.
Expert-level Microsoft security certification covering designing Zero Trust strategy, security operations, identity, infrastructure, and data protection architectures in Microsoft environments.
Covers threat detection and response using Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Defender for Cloud, and Microsoft 365 Defender. Designed for SOC analysts working within the Microsoft security ecosystem.

certs.full_name: Microsoft Identity and Access Administrator Associate
certs.brief: Focuses on designing, implementing, and operating an organization's identity and access management systems using Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD). Covers SSO, MFA, conditional access, and privileged identity management.

certs.full_name: Microsoft Information Protection and Compliance Administrator Associate
certs.brief: Validates expertise in implementing Microsoft Purview information protection, data loss prevention, retention, and compliance solutions. Targets compliance administrators working in Microsoft 365 environments.

certs.full_name: VMware Certified Advanced Professional — Data Center Virtualization
certs.brief: Advanced VMware certification requiring hands-on lab exam skills in deploying and designing vSphere-based data center solutions. Prerequisite track toward the VCDX designation.

certs.full_name: VMware Certified Advanced Professional — Network Virtualization
certs.brief: Advanced VMware certification validating deep hands-on skills in designing and deploying NSX software-defined networking solutions. Part of the path toward VCDX-NV.

certs.full_name: VMware Certified Design Expert — Data Center Virtualization
certs.brief: The highest VMware certification, awarded through a rigorous design defense panel. Validates mastery in designing enterprise-scale vSphere and VMware Cloud Foundation data center solutions.

certs.full_name: VMware Certified Design Expert — Network Virtualization
certs.brief: The pinnacle VMware network virtualization certification, earned through a panel design defense. Validates expert-level skill in designing and architecting large-scale VMware NSX deployments.

certs.full_name: VMware Certified Implementation Expert — Network Virtualization
certs.brief: Expert-level VMware certification (formerly VCAP Deploy track) validating ability to implement and configure complex VMware NSX network virtualization environments. Serves as a VCDX-NV prerequisite.

certs.full_name: VMware Certified Professional — Data Center Virtualization
certs.brief: Core VMware certification validating skills in deploying, managing, and optimizing VMware vSphere environments. A fundamental credential for virtualization and private cloud administrators.
Validates skills in deploying, configuring, and managing VMware NSX network virtualization and software-defined networking solutions. Targets network engineers working with NSX-T/NSX-V.

certs.full_name: GIAC Exploit Researcher and Advanced Penetration Tester
certs.brief: Elite certification covering advanced exploitation, shellcode development, and vulnerability research. One of the most technically demanding GIAC credentials, targeting experienced exploit developers and advanced penetration testers.

Combined elite credential awarded upon passing OSEP, OSED, and OSWE — demonstrating mastery across advanced pentesting, exploit development, and web application exploitation. One of the rarest credentials in offensive security.
Hands-on credential covering Windows exploit development including buffer overflows, SEH exploitation, and ROP chain construction. Targets security researchers and exploit developers.

Elite exploit development credential covering advanced Windows exploitation, kernel bugs, and modern mitigation bypass techniques. Considered one of the most technically demanding certifications in the offensive security field.
Validates proficiency with the FTK (Forensic Toolkit) digital forensics platform for evidence processing and analysis. Recognized in corporate and law enforcement digital forensics roles.
Validates skills in mobile device evidence extraction and physical analysis using Cellebrite tools. Widely used by law enforcement and corporate investigators for mobile forensic casework.
Covers digital forensics methodology, evidence acquisition, file system analysis, and investigation of cybercrime. Targets forensic investigators and law enforcement personnel working cyber cases.

Validates proficiency with the EnCase digital forensics platform for evidence acquisition, analysis, and reporting. Recognized by law enforcement and corporate investigators working with EnCase toolsets.
Advanced forensic analysis of mobile devices including acquisition, analysis, and reporting on iOS and Android artifacts. Targets digital forensic investigators specializing in mobile evidence.
Advanced forensic analysis covering memory forensics, timeline analysis, and investigation of sophisticated attacks. Suited for experienced forensic analysts and DFIR professionals.
Covers Windows forensic examination including file system analysis, registry forensics, browser artifacts, and evidence collection. Targets forensic examiners and incident responders.
Validates skills in network traffic analysis, protocol analysis, and forensic investigation of network-based attacks. Targets forensic analysts and network security investigators.

Validates skills in static and dynamic malware analysis, reverse engineering, and understanding malicious code behavior. Targets malware analysts, threat researchers, and advanced incident responders.

Vendor-specific forensic credential validating use of McAfee/Trellix endpoint forensic tools for investigation and incident response. Suited for security analysts operating in McAfee-centric environments.

Introductory hands-on credential covering malware analysis, static and dynamic analysis techniques, and basic reverse engineering. Suited for entry-level analysts wanting to break into malware research.
Practical digital forensics credential covering evidence acquisition, file system analysis, memory forensics, and forensic reporting. Targets aspiring digital forensic examiners and DFIR analysts.

certs.full_name: BCS Practitioner Certificate in Information Risk Management
certs.brief: BCS credential covering information risk management methodologies including risk assessment, risk treatment, and governance aligned to UK and international standards. Valued in UK public sector, financial services, and regulated industries where BCS certifications carry institutional recognition.

certs.full_name: BCS Foundation Certificate in IT Service Management
certs.brief: BCS foundational credential covering IT service management concepts, frameworks, and best practices for IT professionals in the UK market. Provides grounding in service management principles relevant to help desk, support, and IT operations roles.

Covers the NIST Risk Management Framework (RMF) and the Authorization to Operate (ATO) process for U.S. federal information systems. Requires 2 years of experience and is specifically designed for information security officers and authorization officials in U.S. government and defense contractor contexts.

PMI's entry-level project management certification covering the fundamentals of project management terminology, processes, and the PMBOK framework. Requires 23 hours of project management education and no work experience, making it accessible to early-career professionals and students.

Focuses on control self-assessment methodologies used to evaluate organizational risks and controls through facilitated workshops and structured approaches. Requires 2 years of work experience and is suited for professionals who facilitate CSA programs within organizations.

Technical privacy credential focused on engineering and implementing privacy into technology systems — privacy by design in practice. Requires 3 years of experience and targets software engineers, security engineers, and IT architects working with regulated data.

IIA specialty credential for internal auditors in banking, insurance, and other financial services industries, covering financial services-specific risk and regulatory requirements. Requires 2 years of financial services audit experience.

Specialized IIA credential for auditors working in public sector and government audit environments, covering government-specific accountability standards and audit methodologies. Requires 2 years of government audit experience and is valued in municipal, state, and federal audit offices.

certs.full_name: Certified in Governance of Enterprise IT
certs.brief: Executive-level ISACA credential for CIOs, IT directors, and board advisors responsible for enterprise IT governance. Requires 5 years of IT governance experience and is deeply aligned to the COBIT 2019 framework.

The global gold standard for internal auditors, covering internal audit standards, business acumen, risk-based auditing, and governance. Requires varying experience based on education level and is recognized worldwide as the premier credential for professionals in corporate internal audit functions.

Focuses on privacy program governance and management, including building and operationalizing privacy programs, privacy by design, vendor management, and incident response from a privacy program perspective. Complements the CIPP credentials for professionals managing organizational privacy functions.

Covers Canadian privacy law including PIPEDA, provincial privacy legislation, and Quebec Law 25, providing the knowledge needed to manage privacy programs under Canadian regulatory requirements. Suited for privacy professionals working in or advising Canadian organizations.

Covers European data protection law with a deep focus on GDPR, including lawful bases for processing, data subject rights, international transfers, and supervisory authority requirements. The globally recognized credential for GDPR compliance professionals and Data Protection Officers.

certs.full_name: Certified Information Privacy Professional / United States
certs.brief: IAPP's credential covering U.S. privacy law across federal and state regimes including CCPA/CPRA, HIPAA, FERPA, and emerging state comprehensive privacy laws. The leading credential for privacy professionals working in or with U.S.-based organizations.

Technical privacy credential covering how to embed privacy into technology products and systems, including data minimization, anonymization, consent management, and privacy risk assessment for software and systems. Targeted at IT professionals, developers, and security engineers working at the intersection of privacy and technology.

Gold standard for IT audit professionals, covering audit processes, governance, and information asset protection. Requires 5 years of IS audit experience and is near-mandatory for senior IT auditor roles at Big 4 firms and regulators globally.

certs.full_name: SOC 2 Examination — AICPA Trust Services Criteria
certs.brief: SOC 2 (System and Organization Controls 2) is an AICPA attestation framework covering security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy of service organization systems. CPAs and auditors conduct SOC 2 Type I and Type II examinations, which are widely required by enterprise customers of SaaS and cloud providers.

Designation for candidates who have passed the CISA exam but have not yet met the 5-year experience requirement, allowing early-career professionals to demonstrate exam-level knowledge while accruing the required experience to become certified.

Introductory credential covering the COBIT 2019 IT governance framework, including governance objectives, design factors, and the governance system. Provides the foundational knowledge needed by IT auditors, GRC professionals, and executives implementing or assessing IT governance programs.

The foundational U.S. accounting license, with significant relevance in IT audit and SOX compliance contexts where CPAs conduct financial statement audits and evaluate IT general controls. Requires passing the Uniform CPA Exam and meeting state-specific education and experience requirements.

certs.full_name: Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control
certs.brief: Focuses on IT risk identification, assessment, response, and control implementation aligned to enterprise risk management. Requires 3 years of IT risk experience and is heavily used by GRC analysts and third-party risk managers.

Designation for candidates who pass the CRISC exam before accumulating the required 3 years of IT risk management experience, enabling early-career professionals to demonstrate risk management knowledge while building toward full CRISC certification.

IIA credential focused on providing assurance over risk management frameworks and processes, including ERM, governance, and risk culture assessment. Requires active CIA certification plus 4,500 hours of internal audit or risk management experience.

RIMS's flagship credential for enterprise risk management professionals, covering risk strategy, risk culture, and integration of ERM with organizational decision-making. Requires 3 years of risk management experience and is well-recognized in corporate risk officer and insurance risk roles.

Validates the competencies required to fulfill the Data Protection Officer role mandated by GDPR and other data protection regulations, including oversight of compliance, engagement with supervisory authorities, and advising on data protection impact assessments. Targeted at privacy officers and compliance professionals in European data protection contexts.

IAPP's highest recognition, awarded to privacy professionals who hold multiple IAPP certifications and demonstrate sustained leadership, knowledge, and contribution to the privacy field. Recognized as a mark of distinction for senior privacy officers and leaders.

GIAC credential for security-aware project managers covering project management in the context of IT and security programs, including risk management, stakeholder communication, and security project governance. Suited for project managers working in information security or technology risk environments.

OCEG credential validating competency in the GRC (Governance, Risk, Compliance) Capability Model, covering principled performance, accountability, and integrated GRC program design. Suited for compliance officers, risk managers, and GRC program leads at organizations adopting the OCEG framework.

Entry-level GRC credential from OCEG validating foundational knowledge of governance, risk, and compliance principles aligned to the OCEG GRC Capability Model (Red Book). Designed for professionals new to GRC roles or those seeking a structured introduction to the GRC discipline.

certs.full_name: HealthCare Information Security and Privacy Practitioner
certs.brief: Specialized credential combining information security and privacy principles specifically within the healthcare sector, covering HIPAA, patient data protection, and healthcare-specific risk management. Requires 2 years of healthcare industry experience and targets compliance officers, privacy officers, and security professionals in hospital and health IT environments.

Validates expertise in implementing and managing a Business Continuity Management System (BCMS) in compliance with ISO 22301. Designed for BCM managers, risk managers, and IT continuity professionals responsible for organizational resilience programs.

Introductory credential covering the basic concepts, principles, and requirements of the ISO/IEC 27001 Information Security Management System standard. Designed for professionals new to information security governance who need foundational awareness of the ISMS framework before pursuing Lead Implementer or Lead Auditor.

Covers planning and conducting ISO/IEC 27001 compliance audits against an organization's ISMS, including audit techniques, evidence gathering, and reporting. Intended for auditors, quality managers, and consultants performing third-party or internal ISMS audits.

Validates the ability to plan, implement, manage, and maintain an Information Security Management System (ISMS) in accordance with ISO/IEC 27001. Targets information security managers, consultants, and IT professionals responsible for establishing organizational ISMS programs.

Covers the information security risk management process defined in ISO/IEC 27005, including risk identification, analysis, evaluation, and treatment within an ISMS context. Suited for risk managers and security professionals responsible for structured information security risk assessment programs.

Covers the ISO 31000 international standard for risk management principles, framework, and process applicable across any organization or sector. Suited for enterprise risk managers and ERM program leads who need to demonstrate competency in internationally recognized risk management practice.

Module within the ITIL 4 Managing Professional designation covering the integration of different value streams and activities to create, deliver, and support IT services, including service design, service request management, and monitoring. Required module for ITIL MP designation.

Executive-level ITIL 4 module covering digital business strategy, the impact of digital disruption, and how IT leaders position their organizations to thrive in digital environments. Required for the ITIL 4 Strategic Leader designation and suited for senior IT leaders and CIOs.

Shared module within both ITIL 4 Managing Professional and Strategic Leader designations, covering governance, organizational change management, measurement, and continual improvement at both operational and strategic levels.

ITIL 4 module covering customer and user engagement, relationship management, and the co-creation of value across the customer journey. Part of the ITIL 4 Managing Professional designation, focusing on how service providers develop service relationships to deliver business outcomes.

Foundational credential covering the ITIL 4 service management framework, including key concepts, the service value system, and the four dimensions model. The entry point for IT service management professionals and required prerequisite for all higher ITIL 4 certifications.

ITIL 4 module covering techniques and methods enabling IT organizations to work at high velocity, including DevOps principles, Agile, Lean, and resilient service delivery. Part of the ITIL 4 Managing Professional designation and relevant for IT leaders and practitioners in fast-paced digital transformation environments.

Practitioner-level ITIL 4 designation covering IT teams, workflows, value streams, and direct delivery of IT products and services. Requires completing four modules: Create, Deliver and Support; Drive Stakeholder Value; High Velocity IT; and Direct, Plan and Improve.

The highest ITIL 4 designation, requiring candidates to hold both Managing Professional and Strategic Leader designations and demonstrate the ability to apply ITIL principles and practices in real-world situations through a work-based assignment. Reserved for highly experienced IT service management leaders.

Strategic-level ITIL 4 designation for IT leaders who influence business direction and use IT to create competitive advantage. Requires completing Direct, Plan and Improve and Digital and IT Strategy modules alongside ITIL 4 Foundation.

PMI's agile credential covering multiple agile approaches including Scrum, Kanban, Lean, XP, and SAFe. Requires 2,000 hours of general project experience and 1,500 hours working on agile projects, and is suited for practitioners and coaches working in agile delivery environments.

Covers business analysis skills including needs assessment, requirements elicitation, traceability, and solution evaluation within the project lifecycle. Requires 4,500 hours of business analysis experience and is suited for business analysts, product owners, and requirements engineers.

Specialized PMI credential focused on project risk identification, assessment, response planning, and monitoring. Requires 3 years of project risk management experience and 30 hours of risk management education, and is valued in program management and complex project environments.

Specialized PMI credential for project scheduling professionals, covering schedule development, resource optimization, schedule control, and earned value management. Requires 40 months of project scheduling experience and is valued in program management offices and complex multi-project environments.

The world's leading project management certification, covering predictive (waterfall), agile, and hybrid project delivery approaches. Requires 36 months of project leadership experience and 35 hours of project management education, and is widely required for senior project and program manager roles globally.

PMI's highest-level credential for executives who manage portfolios of programs and projects aligned to organizational strategy. Requires 8 years of professional business experience and 4 years of portfolio management experience.

Advanced PMI certification for professionals managing related projects as a coordinated program to achieve strategic business objectives. Requires 4 years of project management experience and 4 years of program management experience, and is suited for senior program leaders.

IIA's advanced leadership qualification for experienced internal audit professionals aspiring to chief audit executive (CAE) roles, covering strategic thinking, organizational leadership, and stakeholder engagement at the executive level. Requires active CIA certification plus demonstrated leadership experience.

Covers The Open Group Architecture Framework (TOGAF), the most widely adopted enterprise architecture framework, including the Architecture Development Method (ADM) and governance of architecture programs. Widely held by enterprise architects and IT strategy professionals across large organizations.

Covers the Zachman Framework for enterprise architecture, a two-dimensional classification schema for organizing enterprise architecture descriptions across multiple perspectives. Valued by enterprise architects, business analysts, and IT strategists working on large-scale architecture programs.
Professional certification from IDPro validating practitioner-level knowledge of identity and access management principles, standards, and practices. Designed for experienced IAM professionals.

Foundation-level identity certification from IDPro covering core IAM concepts including authentication, authorization, identity governance, and directory services. Targets those entering the IAM field.

certs.full_name: Certified Identity Security Manager (Palo Alto Networks)
certs.brief: Palo Alto Networks specialist credential validating expertise in managing and securing identities using Palo Alto's identity security portfolio including identity-based policy enforcement and user credential protection.

Validates foundational skills in implementing and administering CyberArk Privileged Access Management (PAM) solutions including vaulting, session management, and credential rotation for privileged accounts.

Advanced CyberArk certification validating expert-level skills in architecting and deploying enterprise CyberArk PAM solutions at scale, including complex integrations, high availability, and disaster recovery.

ISACA certification validating expertise in applying data science concepts, tools, and methods within an IT governance and assurance context. Relevant for IAM analytics, risk, and compliance roles involving identity data.

Validates skills in configuring and administering the Okta Identity Cloud including provisioning, application integration, security policies, and Okta Workflows. For IAM admins managing Okta deployments.
Advanced Okta certification for consultants and architects validating the ability to design and implement complex Okta identity solutions, custom integrations, and enterprise-scale IAM architectures.

Foundation-level Okta certification covering core Okta platform concepts including user lifecycle management, SSO, MFA, and integration with common applications. Entry point to the Okta certification path.
Validates hands-on expertise in deploying and administering SailPoint IdentityIQ for on-premises identity governance, access certifications, role management, and provisioning workflows.
Vendor certification validating ability to implement, configure, and manage SailPoint IdentityNow (Identity Security Cloud) for cloud-based identity governance and administration.

Covers forensic investigation and incident response within HIPAA-regulated healthcare environments. Targets healthcare security professionals responsible for breach investigation and regulatory compliance.
Covers host-based forensic analysis and intrusion investigation including memory analysis and artifact examination. Targets incident response analysts working host-based investigations.
Validates incident handling and response skills including preparation, detection, containment, and recovery. Suited for security analysts and incident response team members.
Validates skills in detecting, responding to, and recovering from security incidents. Covers attack techniques, containment strategies, and incident response methodology.

certs.full_name: GIAC Defending Advanced Threats and Forensics Incident Response
certs.brief: Combines advanced threat defense and forensic incident response skills for seasoned DFIR practitioners. Targets senior incident handlers and forensic analysts responding to sophisticated intrusions.
Focuses on incident response and defense for industrial control systems (ICS) and OT environments. Targets security professionals protecting critical infrastructure.

Covers incident response methodology, threat hunting, and forensic analysis from an offensive security perspective. Targets analysts who want to understand attacker techniques to improve their response capabilities.

certs.full_name: Advanced Incident Response, Threat Hunting, and Digital Forensics (FOR508 course cert)
certs.brief: Advanced incident response and threat hunting training credential covering memory forensics, timeline analysis, and hunting for sophisticated attackers across enterprise environments.

certs.full_name: Hacker Tools, Techniques, and Incident Handling (SEC504 course cert)
certs.brief: Covers attacker techniques and the incident handling process, teaching defenders to think offensively. Foundational for security analysts and incident responders wanting to understand and counter real attack methods.
Hands-on incident response credential covering detection, containment, eradication, and recovery processes. Targets security analysts and incident response team members building practical IR skills.
Expert-level Cisco certification focused on large-scale network infrastructure design, including security architecture. One of the most advanced Cisco design credentials available.

certs.full_name: Cisco Certified Internetwork Expert Enterprise Infrastructure
certs.brief: Expert-level Cisco certification covering advanced enterprise networking including SD-WAN, network assurance, security, and automation. Requires both a written exam and a rigorous hands-on lab exam.

The most prestigious Cisco security certification, requiring both a written qualifying exam and an 8-hour hands-on lab exam. Validates expert-level skills in designing, deploying, and troubleshooting complex security solutions.
Entry-level Cisco certification covering IP connectivity, routing, switching, security fundamentals, and automation. One of the most recognized networking certs in the industry.

Associate-level cert focused on security operations center (SOC) skills including security monitoring, host-based analysis, network intrusion analysis, and security policies. Targets entry-level SOC analyst roles.

Professional-level Cisco certification for implementing and operating core Cisco security technologies including firewalls, VPNs, IDS/IPS, and secure access. Requires passing a core exam plus one concentration exam.
The highest tier of Fortinet's Network Security Expert program, validating mastery of the Fortinet Security Fabric across complex multi-vendor enterprise and service provider environments.
Entry-level Juniper security certification covering fundamentals of the Junos OS and Juniper SRX Series firewalls. Validates foundational knowledge of network security concepts on Juniper platforms.
The highest-level Juniper security certification, validated through a hands-on lab exam covering advanced Juniper SRX firewall configurations, IPS, VPN, and security policy management at scale.
Professional-level Juniper security cert validating advanced skills in deploying and troubleshooting Juniper security platforms including SRX firewalls, IPS, and VPN technologies.
Vendor-neutral entry-level networking certification covering infrastructure, network operations, security, and troubleshooting. A widely accepted baseline for IT networking roles.

certs.full_name: Palo Alto Networks Certified Cloud Network Security Engineer
certs.brief: Specialist certification validating expertise in deploying and managing Palo Alto Networks cloud network security solutions including Prisma Cloud and VM-Series firewalls in cloud environments.

certs.full_name: Palo Alto Networks Certified Cybersecurity Associate
certs.brief: Foundation-level Palo Alto Networks certification covering cybersecurity concepts, network security, cloud security basics, and security operations. Designed for those new to cybersecurity.

certs.full_name: Palo Alto Networks Certified Cloud Security Engineer
certs.brief: Validates hands-on expertise with Prisma Cloud for securing cloud infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Covers cloud workload protection, compliance, and cloud security posture management.

certs.full_name: Palo Alto Networks Certified Network Security Engineer
certs.brief: The flagship Palo Alto Networks certification, validating deep expertise in designing, deploying, configuring, maintaining, and troubleshooting Palo Alto Networks next-generation firewalls.

Hands-on web application security certification requiring candidates to exploit real vulnerabilities using Burp Suite in a time-limited exam. Recognized by web application security professionals and bug bounty hunters.

Broad ethical hacking certification covering attack phases, common tools, and vulnerability exploitation techniques. Widely listed on government and enterprise job postings, though criticized by practitioners for its multiple-choice exam format.
Combined designation awarded upon passing both the CEH knowledge exam and the CEH Practical. Demonstrates both theoretical understanding and hands-on ethical hacking capability.
Hands-on companion to the CEH, requiring candidates to perform real attacks in a live lab environment. Adds practical credibility to the standard CEH credential.
Hands-on penetration testing credential covering network, web, IoT, and OT environments in a live range. Passing with high marks simultaneously awards the LPT Master designation.

certs.full_name: CREST Certified Infrastructure Tester / Certified Web Application Tester
certs.brief: Senior-level CREST penetration testing qualification covering advanced infrastructure or web application testing techniques. Required for high-assurance UK government and financial sector penetration testing engagements.

UK-based penetration testing credential validating foundational penetration testing knowledge across infrastructure and web application testing. Required by many UK government and financial sector contracts.
Validates management and planning skills for red team and CBEST/TIBER-style simulated attack engagements. Targets senior red team managers leading advanced threat simulation programs.

certs.full_name: GIAC Assessing and Auditing Wireless Networks
certs.brief: Validates wireless network security assessment skills including 802.11 attacks, WPA/WPA2 cracking, and rogue access point detection. Designed for penetration testers assessing wireless environments.

Validates Python scripting skills applied to security testing, including writing exploit scripts, automating reconnaissance, and building security tooling. Targets penetration testers who want to extend their scripting capabilities.
Covers security assessment of mobile applications and devices on iOS and Android platforms. Designed for penetration testers and security analysts working in mobile environments.
Validates penetration testing skills including reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, and reporting. Covers the full pentest methodology and is widely recognized in the industry.
Advanced red team operations credential covering adversary simulation, command and control, and advanced evasion techniques aligned with MITRE ATT&CK. Targets experienced red team operators.
Demonstrates skills in web application security testing including OWASP Top 10 exploitation, injection attacks, and authentication flaws. Suited for web application security testers.

certs.full_name: eLearnSecurity Web Application Penetration Tester eXtreme
certs.brief: Advanced web application penetration testing credential covering complex vulnerability chaining, advanced injection attacks, and sophisticated evasion techniques. Targets experienced web application security testers.

Advanced practical penetration testing credential requiring candidates to conduct a full pentest engagement against a complex multi-layered network. Targets senior penetration testers seeking EC-Council's highest offensive designation.

24-hour hands-on lab exam requiring candidates to compromise multiple machines and submit a professional pentest report. Widely considered the gold standard practical credential for penetration testers.

Advanced penetration testing credential focused on evading modern defenses, bypassing AV/EDR, and attacking hardened enterprise environments. Targets experienced pentesters looking to level up their offensive toolkit.

Advanced web application security credential focusing on source code review, white-box testing, and chaining vulnerabilities for full application compromise. Targets experienced web application penetration testers.

Hands-on wireless security certification covering 802.11 attacks, WEP/WPA cracking, and rogue access point attacks. Practical exam requiring candidates to compromise wireless networks in a live environment.
Entry-level hands-on penetration testing credential covering internal network assessment fundamentals and basic Active Directory attacks. Designed for beginners transitioning into offensive security.
Practical mobile application security testing credential covering iOS and Android app assessment methodology. Targets penetration testers expanding into mobile application security.

Five-day hands-on practical exam simulating a real-world network penetration test including Active Directory attacks and a professional report submission. A highly respected practitioner-focused alternative to CEH for those on a budget.
Vendor-neutral penetration testing certification covering planning, scoping, information gathering, attacks, and reporting. Meets DoD 8570 requirements and targets mid-level penetration testers.
Intermediate practical penetration testing credential covering network pentesting, web application attacks, Active Directory, and report writing. Often used as a stepping stone between eJPT and OSCP.

Advanced penetration testing credential covering red team operations, advanced Active Directory attacks, and evasion techniques. Targets experienced penetration testers ready to advance to red team engagements.

Entry-level hands-on penetration testing certification covering basic network attacks, web application testing, and host-based exploitation. Widely recommended as a first practical credential before pursuing OSCP.
Practical mobile application penetration testing credential covering Android and iOS app security assessment. Targets penetration testers working in mobile security assessment roles.

Hands-on web application penetration testing certification covering OWASP vulnerabilities, injection attacks, and authentication bypass. Targets penetration testers specializing in web application security.

Focuses on integrating security practices throughout the software development lifecycle, from requirements and design through testing and deployment. Requires 4 years of SDLC experience and targets software developers, architects, and security professionals involved in application security.
Advanced practitioner-level credential covering enterprise security architecture, risk management, and technical integration of security controls. Targets senior security engineers and architects.

Covers application security assessment skills including code review, dynamic testing, and vulnerability identification in software. Suited for application security testers and secure development professionals.

Covers cryptographic algorithms, PKI, encryption standards, and their practical security implications. Suited for security professionals who need a solid cryptography foundation in their assessment or engineering work.

Covers advanced penetration testing methodology including pen testing frameworks, documentation, and reporting. Positioned as the next step after CEH for security analysts focused on structured assessments.

Covers cloud security assessment fundamentals including misconfigurations, identity and access management risks, and cloud-native attack paths. Suited for penetration testers and security analysts working in cloud environments.

Covers vulnerability management program design, assessment methodologies, and remediation prioritization at enterprise scale. Targets vulnerability management engineers and security analysts responsible for continuous assessment.
Entry-level GIAC credential covering core security concepts, networking basics, and foundational security practices. Suited for non-technical professionals entering the information security field.
Covers open-source intelligence gathering techniques used in reconnaissance and investigations. Suited for penetration testers, investigators, and threat intelligence analysts.

Validates skills in assessing and securing AWS, Azure, and GCP environments including cloud-specific attack techniques and defense strategies. Targets cloud security engineers and penetration testers.
Validates skills in network and endpoint defense, malware detection, and security operations. Targets security practitioners responsible for defending enterprise environments.

Broad foundational certification covering active defense, network security, cryptography, and incident handling. Ideal for security professionals moving from general IT into a dedicated security role.

certs.full_name: GIAC Strategic Planning, Policy, and Leadership
certs.brief: Covers security strategy, policy development, and leadership for senior security practitioners. Targets CISOs and security directors responsible for organizational security programs.

Industry-standard entry-level security certification covering foundational security concepts, threats, vulnerabilities, and security controls. Required by DoD 8570 and widely used as a baseline credential for security roles.

Hands-on defensive security certification covering phishing analysis, threat intelligence, SIEM investigation, and digital forensics basics. Designed as an entry point for aspiring SOC analysts and blue team practitioners.

Advanced hands-on blue team credential covering threat hunting, advanced SIEM analysis, malware analysis, and incident response. Targets experienced SOC analysts and blue teamers looking to advance their skills.
Validates advanced threat intelligence management skills for leading intelligence programs at strategic, operational, and tactical levels. Targets senior threat intelligence professionals.

Covers network defense strategies, threat detection, and security operations from a blue team perspective. Designed for network administrators and security engineers responsible for protecting network infrastructure.
Validates network intrusion analysis skills including traffic analysis, pattern recognition, and investigation of network-based attacks. Targets SOC analysts and network security investigators.

Covers threat detection, behavioral analytics, and security operations center (SOC) practices. Targets security analysts responsible for monitoring and responding to threats in enterprise environments.
Covers perimeter defense, firewall architecture, packet filtering, and network access control. Suited for network security engineers and firewall administrators.
Validates skills in gathering, analyzing, and applying threat intelligence to defend organizations. Targets threat intelligence analysts and senior SOC professionals.
Advanced credential focused on detecting and responding to sophisticated nation-state-level threat actors. Targets senior blue team practitioners and security engineers at mature organizations.
Validates continuous security monitoring skills including SIEM, log analysis, network traffic analysis, and security tool operations. Designed for SOC analysts and security engineers.
Covers defensive security analysis from an attacker's perspective, including log analysis, SIEM usage, and detection of offensive techniques. Bridges the gap between red and blue team thinking.
Government-approved foundational security certification required for many DoD and federal contractor IA roles. Covers threats, vulnerabilities, architecture, and security operations fundamentals.

Validates Splunk administration skills including configuration, data onboarding, user management, and platform maintenance. Targets security engineers responsible for maintaining Splunk infrastructure in a SOC.

Entry-level Splunk credential covering basic data search, reporting, and dashboard creation within the Splunk platform. Suitable for analysts beginning to work with Splunk for log analysis and security monitoring.

Covers advanced Splunk searches, field extractions, transforming commands, and creating complex visualizations. Targets analysts who need deeper Splunk capabilities for security investigations and SOC operations.

Advanced Splunk credential covering the Splunk Enterprise Security (ES) SIEM, including correlation searches, threat intelligence integration, and incident review workflows. Targets SIEM engineers and senior SOC analysts.

Covers network defense from a penetration tester's perspective, bridging offensive and defensive security skills. Suited for analysts who want to strengthen detection capabilities by understanding attacker methods.

ASIS entry-level credential for early-career security professionals demonstrating foundational knowledge of security principles and practices. Requires passing an examination without extensive experience requirements, providing a path for students and those new to the security management profession.

Designation granted to candidates who pass a qualifying ISC2 exam (CISSP or SSCP) but have not yet met the experience requirement. Allows early-career professionals to demonstrate exam-level knowledge while accumulating the years of experience needed to upgrade to full certification status.

Executive-level credential from EC-Council focused on the five domains of CISO-level leadership: governance, security risk management, controls, audit and testing, finance, and strategic program management. Requires 5 years of senior-level IS management experience and targets current or aspiring CISOs.

Vendor-neutral cloud security credential covering cloud architecture, data security, platform security, legal compliance, and operations. Requires 5 years of IT experience including 3 in security and 1 in cloud, and is the leading vendor-neutral cloud security certification for architects and engineers.

Management-focused security credential covering governance, risk management, program management, and incident response. Requires 5 years of security management experience and is the primary stepping stone to CISO roles.

Designation for candidates who pass the CISM exam but have not yet accumulated the required 5 years of security management experience. Allows aspiring security managers to formalize their knowledge while building toward full CISM certification.

The most globally recognized senior security credential, covering 8 domains from risk management and asset security to software development security. Requires 5 years of experience across two or more domains and is widely required for senior security architect, manager, and CISO roles.

The gold standard credential in physical security and security management, covering security programs, investigations, personnel security, and emergency management. Requires 9 years of security management experience (reduced with a degree) and is widely recognized in corporate security director and CSO roles.

Validates expertise in designing, implementing, and managing security awareness programs including behavior change methodologies, phishing simulation, and metrics. Suited for security awareness program managers and security culture advocates within organizations.

Performance-based, hands-on cybersecurity credential delivered in a live lab environment covering Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, and Recover functions. Designed for SOC analysts, incident responders, and security engineers who want to prove practical skill rather than test-taking ability.

Credential focused on information security management within the European regulatory landscape, covering NIS2 Directive requirements, GDPR obligations, and EU-specific cybersecurity governance frameworks. Relevant for security managers and compliance professionals at organizations subject to EU information security regulations.

ASIS credential focused on physical security assessment, design, and implementation including threat and vulnerability assessments, security systems, and loss prevention. Requires 5 years of physical security experience and is suited for physical security practitioners and consultants.

Senior-level information security leadership credential from the Institute of Information Security Professionals (IISP) validating strategic security leadership, governance, and program management capabilities. Targeted at current and aspiring CISOs and senior security directors.

Covers the SABSA framework for risk-driven security architecture, linking business requirements through logical, physical, and operational security architecture layers. Offered at Foundation, Practitioner, and Master levels, and is favored by security architects designing enterprise security programs aligned to business strategy.

ISC2's practitioner-level credential for IT and security operations staff covering access controls, cryptography, network security, and incident response. Requires only 1 year of experience and serves as an effective stepping stone toward CISSP.
